scopeRule: Schedule only roster entries confirmed by current repository data. Future Map 2/3 or unconfirmed secret units require a later current-data pass before entering a wave.
prerequisites: Slime/Golem migration batch used to validate the batch workflow
each unit entering production has an approved current source/neutral identity

executionOrder.goal: prove two-anatomy batch planning, common validation, combined review, partial approval and grouped migration without forcing one motion method
measure real review/redo throughput and adjust batch-size guidelines
freeze common validator config shape, artifact set, package isolation and approval overlay conventions
use a moderate 3-6 unit archetype-driven batch rather than another single-character pilot
run Idle/Move batch work across compatible units while smaller attack sub-batches proceed independently

executionOrder.step: Hold complex motions for dedicated passes
executionOrder.goal: bosses, first-of-archetype quadruped, signature skills and failed packages stay out of fast-path batches

waves.purpose: Pilot migration candidates immediately after Archer acceptance
waves.batchPlan: Shared source audit, common technical validation and combined review; anatomy-specific motion generation remains separate.
waves.notes: Slime conditional-reuse path; Golem replacement path.

waves.purpose: Establish reusable biped melee Idle/Move and several basic-attack biomechanics subgroups
waves.batchPlan: Idle 4-unit batch; Move 4-unit batch; attacks split into fist/blade/heavy sub-batches instead of one generic attack.

waves.purpose: Reuse biped locomotion while proving small semantic attack groups for caster/ranged/support units
waves.batchPlan: Idle/Move may batch broadly; attacks split by cast/command/holy/gadget/bow semantics using existing Class 1 profiles and ranged-monster gates.

waves.purpose: Fast-unit locomotion and sharp attack readability
waves.batchPlan: Idle/Move batch-with-review; attack sub-batches max 2-3 based on weapon/anatomy. Ninja enters only after exact source identity approval.
waves.runtimeBoundary: Warp/backline targeting remains runtime-owned, never baked into motion.

waves.purpose: Scale proven humanoid melee pipelines into Class 2
waves.batchPlan: Idle/Move grouped; attacks split by shield/heavy/blade/hybrid semantics.

waves.purpose: Scale ranged/caster pipelines with existing range/role data
waves.batchPlan: Idle batches up to 6; Move 3-6; attack/cast groups 2-3 only when semantics/weapon biomechanics match.

waves.purpose: Small deliberate batch for specialist/support identities that do not fit a large generic attack group
waves.batchPlan: Share biped technical pipeline; character-specific attack/skill review remains stricter.
waves.whyOnlyTwo: Avoid padding a batch with biomechanically unrelated units merely to hit a numeric target.

separateDedicatedPasses.reason: First confirmed Map 1 quadruped; individual archetype pilot first, then future beasts can use fast-path if accepted.
Scale/anatomy/telegraph/signature mechanics justify individual or very small dedicated production; common technical tooling still reused.

notScheduled.reason: obsolete in current Map 1 encounter policy
require explicit current gameplay/runtime need before motion production

notScheduled.scope: Map 2/Map 3 future-only or unconfirmed secret roster
notScheduled.reason: do not manufacture production scope without current repository data/identity approval

throughputModel.nature: planning estimate, not a delivery promise and not measured historical throughput
assumptions: exact source/neutral identities are already approved before motion batch starts
common validator/config/artifact tooling exists and is stable
no runtime/Core Logic change is needed per package
standard review artifacts are generated automatically
human review is performed in combined mobile-friendly batch views
packages remain path/hash isolated so partial batch approval is possible

coreUnitsPerDayAfterIdentityReady.definition: Equivalent units with the three core packages Idle+Move+BasicAttack moving through production/technical validation capacity; approval may lag behind production.

humanReviewCapacity.bottleneckRule: Do not increase production batch size if the user cannot clearly compare motion at phone/board scale in one session.

redoAllowance.CONSERVATIVE: 25-35% of packages need at least one correction pass
redoAllowance.COMPLEX_OR_NEW_ARCHETYPE: 30-50% possible; do not use fast-path estimate

technicalVsReviewBottleneck.technical: Common validators/artifacts should make simple technical checks mostly batch-linear and parallelizable.
technicalVsReviewBottleneck.human: Visual review becomes the practical bottleneck; combined pages and partial approval prevent it from becoming all-or-nothing.

timeSavingModel.exampleScope: 30 simple-to-medium core motion packages with identities already approved
timeSavingModel.serialPilotStyle: approximately 20-30 working days if each package repeats planning/handoff/PR/review cycles and effective throughput averages roughly 1-1.5 approved packages/day
timeSavingModel.targetBatchWorkflow: approximately 8-13 working days including technical validation, review scheduling and normal redo allowance
timeSavingModel.fastPathProvenArchetypes: approximately 6-9 working days when most packages are simple, validator/artifact tooling is stable and redo remains low
timeSavingModel.expectedProductionThroughputImprovement: roughly 2.5x-4x for simple/medium repeated archetypes; unique bosses/skills improve less
timeSavingModel.expectedCoordinationPROverheadReduction: roughly 60-80% versus repeating pilot-style planning/readiness PR layers per package
timeSavingModel.warning: These are capacity-planning ranges, not promises. Source-identity generation/approval delays, complex visual failures or new archetypes can dominate elapsed time.

parallelizationRules: Parallelize Idle and Move production across units with approved identities and compatible archetype configs.
Allow Basic Attack sub-batches to proceed independently from broader Idle/Move batches.
Do not wait for one failed package to block sibling packages that passed and remain isolated.
Do not parallelize runtime changes with unapproved asset production.
Keep complex boss/skill work in dedicated passes so it cannot stall simple fast-path waves.
